insights.title: Preset loading stops the arpeggiator via allNotesOff
insights.summary: Loading a user preset sends an allNotesOff message which kills all voices and stops the arpeggiator. This is confirmed by the author as unavoidable at the engine level. To preserve a held note across a preset change, the note must be stored and retriggered manually after load.

insights.title: Hold attribute can be driven by sustain pedal via onController callback
insights.summary: The Hold button activates as soon as it is toggled. To replicate traditional sustain-pedal hold behaviour, set `Arpeggiator.Hold` in the `onController` callback when CC64 crosses the midpoint. Sending artificial sustain pedal messages is not recommended as it may interfere with other processors.

insights.title: Direction traverses insertion order, not pitch order, unless SortKeys is enabled
insights.summary: The Up/Down/UpDown directions operate on the order notes were pressed (insertion order), not ascending pitch order. Only enabling SortKeys causes the arp to traverse notes by pitch. This surprises users who expect 'Up' to always play low-to-high regardless of playing order.

insights.title: Length value of 100 triggers tie mode, not a full-length normal note
insights.summary: Setting the length SliderPack to exactly 100 activates tie/sustain mode (extends note into the next step) rather than playing a note at 100% of step duration. To play a note at near-full length without triggering tie, use 99. This is separate from the EXISTING note that EnableTieNotes requires toggle and 100% length - the length slider alone at 100 triggers it.
insights.verify_hint: Search for length == 100 or length >= 100 condition in the arpeggiator hardcoded script; confirm 100 routes to tie path regardless of EnableTieNotes

insights.title: Artificial arp notes are invisible to sibling MIDI processors
insights.summary: Due to an internal recursion guard, notes generated by the arpeggiator cannot be intercepted by MIDI script processors that are siblings of the arp in the same chain. To process arp-generated notes in a script, place the arpeggiator in the root container so child sound generators' MIDI chains can see the events.
insights.verify_hint: Search for the recursion guard or artificial event flag in hi_scripting MidiProcessor or ArpeggiatorProcessor; confirm sibling restriction

insights.title: Arpeggiator does not stop when the DAW transport stops
insights.summary: The arpeggiator timer continues running when the DAW is stopped; it only stops when held notes are released. Users reported this issue in 2021 and it remained unresolved as of 2025. A manual workaround is to use a TransportHandler to detect stop events and call allNotesOff or bypass the arp.
insights.verify_hint: Check ArpeggiatorProcessor for transport listener or DAW stop handling; grep for TransportListener or isPlaying in the arp implementation

insights.title: Key range filter for arp output requires downstream channel-filter script
insights.summary: The arpeggiator has no built-in input or output key range filter. The recommended workaround is to route arp output to a dedicated MIDI channel (OutputChannel) and add a script processor downstream that ignores notes outside the desired range on that channel.

insights.title: Semitone SliderPack range is adjustable on a linked UI SliderPack
insights.summary: The built-in semitone slider pack defaults to -24 to +24, but the range can be changed by connecting a UI SliderPack to the arp's complex data slot and adjusting the Min/Max properties in the property editor. The arp will then use the custom range.

insights.title: Arp rate drag had a step-2 typo skipping every even tempo value
insights.summary: A bug in the arpeggiator's Tempo parameter caused drag operations to increment by 2 instead of 1, skipping every even tempo value (e.g. 1/16 was unreachable by dragging). Fixed in HISE develop branch. No preset backwards-compatibility issue since the underlying values were unaffected.

insights.title: HISE_USE_EXTENDED_TEMPO_VALUES=1 unlocks additional tempo divisions
insights.summary: The arpeggiator's tempo parameter only shows extended divisions (beyond the default set) when HISE is compiled with `HISE_USE_EXTENDED_TEMPO_VALUES=1` in Extra Preprocessor Definitions. This must be set in both the HISE build and the project's Projucer settings.

insights.title: Arp pattern cannot be exported or dragged to DAW
insights.summary: Unlike the MIDI Player module, the arpeggiator generates notes internally at runtime and has no mechanism to export its pattern as a MIDI file or drag it to the DAW's timeline. The only alternative for exportable sequences is to use the MIDI Player module.

insights.title: OctaveRange can produce notes above MIDI note 127 with no defined clamp behaviour
insights.summary: When OctaveRange is set to a positive value and the user plays high notes, the expanded sequence can generate MIDI note numbers above 127. The handling of out-of-range notes is undefined and community discussion confirms no clamping or wrapping logic has been documented or implemented.
insights.verify_hint: Search ArpeggiatorProcessor note generation for clamping of note numbers after octave expansion; check if note > 127 is guarded

insights.title: The built-in arpeggiator is a hardcoded script; roll your own for customisation
insights.summary: The author has stated that the built-in arp is effectively a hardcoded HiseScript and that any advanced customisation (custom step logic, advanced tie behaviour, key range) is better achieved by writing a custom arpeggiator in HiseScript from scratch. A clock-synced arp snippet is available in the snippet database as a starting point.
